description:
## Scope

main.rs:71–84 (the pattern_check survivor-set construction inside
`validate_specs_or_exit`):

Today:
```rust
let mut reduced = module.clone();
ir_reducer::reduce_module(&mut reduced);
let mt_reduced = ir_typer::type_module(&reduced);
let survivors: HashSet<(String, usize)> = mt_reduced
    .reachable_specs
    .iter()
    .filter_map(|(fid, _)| {
        let &idx = reduced.fn_idx.get(fid)?;
        let f = &reduced.fns[idx];
        let arity = f.block(f.entry).params.len();
        Some((f.name.clone(), arity))
    })
    .collect();
```

After:
```rust
let mut reduced = module.clone();
ir_reducer::reduce_module(&mut reduced);
let reachable = ir_callgraph::reachable_fns(&reduced);
let survivors: HashSet<(String, usize)> = reachable
    .iter()
    .filter_map(|fid| {
        let &idx = reduced.fn_idx.get(fid)?;
        let f = &reduced.fns[idx];
        let arity = f.block(f.entry).params.len();
        Some((f.name.clone(), arity))
    })
    .collect();
```

The `type_module(&reduced)` call is deleted. Net: front-end gate goes
from 2 typer passes to 1.

## Parity risk

`mt_reduced.reachable_specs` is type-narrowed: the typer's worklist
drops specs the produces/holders BFS at ir_typer.rs:767–783 can't
reach. `reachable_fns` is a pure syntactic call-graph walk — strict
superset at the FnId level.

Failure mode: a fn syntactically called in the post-reduce IR whose
every spec is dead at the type level. Today's behavior: skipped from
survivors → no pattern-check warning. New behavior: included →
warning fires.

This is arguably more correct (the body exists in the post-reduce IR
and a `:function_clause` halt could fire at runtime), but it is a
behavior change.

## Verification

- Full fixture matrix run with diff against pre-change pattern_check
  output.
- Any fixture whose pattern-warnings change must be reviewed: either
  the new warning is correct (accept and update golden), or there's a
  real false-positive that needs `reachable_fns` to be narrowed.
- Document the verdict per affected fixture in the PR description.

## Depends on

fz-0z4.A (ir_callgraph module + reachable_fns).

## Acceptance

- `cargo build` clean.
- Fixture matrix parity confirmed (with documented per-fixture
  verdicts for any diff).
- One `type_module` call remains in the front-end gate (the raw-IR
  one, for spec_check).
title: Pattern_check uses reachable_fns on the reduced module — drop second type_module
